scope: One explicit driver registry (internal/driver/registry) becomes the single resolution authority for loop dispatch — explicit prefix -> driver table, fail-fast role checking with errors that name what IS registered, dispatch-free enumeration with availability probing and proxy-routing visibility — plus the sworn#31 prefix rename (openai/ -> Responses API) landed at the model.NewClient choke point, the codex ErrDriverNotImplemented stub removed, and the hand-maintained capabilityRegistry deleted with a new `sworn capabilities` verb rendering registry enumeration.

test_results.command: go test -timeout 300s ./... (full suite, fresh cache — 45 packages ok, 0 FAIL)

reachability.evidence: Built the sworn binary from this branch and ran `SWORN_DIRECT=1 sworn capabilities` (2026-07-10): the verb resolves through the command registry (init() self-registration) and renders the live registry enumeration — all four drivers (claude-subprocess claude-cli/, codex-subprocess codex/, oai-responses-inprocess openai/ + openai-responses/ marked as deprecated alias, oai-inprocess with the eight chat-capable prefixes), roles implementer,verifier, availability probes firing for real (both CLI binaries found on PATH with 'login not probed' detail; in-process identities unavailable with no keys and no proxy login), and the sworn#31 prefix-semantics footer. cmd/sworn/capabilities_test.go:TestCapabilitiesRendersRegistry re-runs this end-to-end through command.Lookup (the integration point main.dispatch resolves from, Rule 1) on every test run. The loop itself does not consume Resolve until S06 (out of scope: 'RunSlice/scheduler consumption of the registry') — the registry package plus the capabilities verb are the affordances this slice owns.
reachability.gate_output: git diff 20dc2dc..HEAD | sworn verify --spec spec.json --diff - --proof proof.json --verifier-model claude-cli/sonnet -> {"verdict":"PASS"} (2026-07-10; model-backed gate run through the keyless claude-cli driver — no API keys/proxy login in this environment, deepseek unavailable)

delivered.item: AC-01: internal/driver/registry provides Default(cfg) explicitly registering the four compiled-in drivers with their prefix mappings, and Resolve(modelID, role) as the single resolution authority; capabilityRegistry deleted (internal/model/registry.go removed, zero remaining references); `sworn capabilities` renders registry enumeration
delivered.evidence: internal/driver/registry/registry_test.go:TestDefaultRegistryTable; git rm internal/model/registry.go in this diff + grep -r capabilityRegistry returns nothing; cmd/sworn/capabilities_test.go:TestCapabilitiesRendersRegistry

delivered.item: AC-02: Resolve with an unregistered prefix errors BEFORE any dispatch, naming the unknown prefix AND enumerating every registered prefix (sorted, aliases marked)
delivered.evidence: internal/driver/registry/registry_test.go:TestResolveUnknownPrefix (asserts the full registered list in the error text); fakeDriver.Dispatch panics if the registry ever dispatches

delivered.item: AC-03: role fail-fast — the error names the driver, the missing role, its declared roles, and which registered drivers DO declare the role ('(none)' when nobody does); no fallback driver is ever returned
delivered.evidence: internal/driver/registry/registry_test.go:TestResolveRoleFailFast (compiled-in table variant + fake-driver variant asserting the declaring-driver enumeration and nil driver return)

delivered.item: AC-04: openai/ -> Responses driver, openai-completions/ -> legacy chat/completions, openai-responses/ -> deprecated alias resolving with a deprecation warning; codex ErrDriverNotImplemented stub removed; AGENTS.md + `sworn capabilities` output + main.go usage text document the new prefixes
delivered.evidence: internal/driver/registry/registry_test.go:TestResolvePrefixRename (captured warning); internal/model/provider_test.go:TestNewClientOpenAIIsResponses, TestNewClientOpenAICompletions; internal/model/cli_test.go:TestNewClientCodexRemoved; AGENTS.md Layout section prefix table; cmd/sworn/main.go usage() capabilities block

delivered.item: AC-05: Drivers() enumerates name, prefixes, RoleSet, availability (binary-on-PATH / key-presence / credentials-file probes, no dispatch possible by construction), and ViaProxy listing which prefixes resolve through the proxy when login is active and SWORN_DIRECT unset — keyed on the per-prefix account.Endpoint check, the same condition FromEnv routes on
delivered.evidence: internal/driver/registry/registry_test.go:TestDriversEnumeration (injected probes flip Available with no HTTP server in existence), TestEnumerationShowsProxyRouting (real credentials file: ViaProxy populated for all nine in-process prefixes, empty under SWORN_DIRECT=1, always empty for subprocess drivers)

delivered.item: AC-06: go build ./... and go test ./internal/driver/... ./internal/model/... ./cmd/sworn/... pass (plus the full suite per the project's cross-package-fixture hazard)
delivered.evidence: test_results in this bundle, fresh cache

delivered.item: Coach ack pin 3: D2 prefix breadth and D3 choke-point rename recorded as Type-2 noted defaults in status.json design_decisions at the in_progress transition

Coach ack pin 4: registry<->NewClient prefix-table consistency test — every registered in-process prefix (aliases included) asserts model.NewClient constructs the wire family the registered identity claims (Responses struct vs chat-capable)
Coach ack flag (b): subprocess availability detail says 'binary found on PATH; login not probed' — never implies dispatch-readiness
delivered.evidence: internal/driver/registry/registry.go:binaryProbe; live `sworn capabilities` output in reachability evidence

delivered.item: Coach ack flag (c): ViaProxy keyed on the per-prefix account.Endpoint(creds, prefix) check, not a blanket login flag

Coach ack flag (d): both pre-existing fused comment lines fixed in passing (config.go proxy-comment line, provider.go ProviderConfig closing-brace line); fused-line grep + gofmt -l + go vet clean over the changed files; full suite green
delivered.evidence: internal/model/config.go:63 area, internal/model/provider.go:33 area in this diff; test_results

delivered.item: FromEnv utility path re-keyed to the new semantics: proxy block selects OpenAIResponses for openai/ and openai-responses/, *OAI for openai-completions/; keyless block drops codex (claude-cli is the only keyless model.Verifier); openai-completions key check shares SWORN_OPENAI_API_KEY
delivered.evidence: internal/model/oai_test.go:TestFromEnvProxyOpenAIIsResponses (new), migrated proxy tests (TestFromEnvUsesProxy/BypassProxy/ProxyDefaultHost/ProxyOverrideWarns/InsufficientCredits/NoCredsUnchanged now exercise openai-completions/ for the *OAI leg)

not_delivered.item: Proxy-aware DISPATCH for the in-process drivers the registry resolves to (InProcess -> model.NewClient is proxy-blind today, so what AC-05's enumeration advertises is not yet honoured by Driver.Dispatch)
not_delivered.reason: Cross-slice contract gap surfaced by the Captain (review.md pin 1, escalate) and ruled by the Coach: S05 does NOT widen to inprocess.go; the gap is owned by S06-loop-dispatch-rewire spec risk R-04 (committed e2b5472 on release-wt), which binds registry-dispatched in-process drivers to resolve their client via the FromEnv-equivalent path with a test that enumeration and dispatch evaluate one shared predicate. The track merges as one unit so the intermediate state never reaches the integration branch.

divergence: AC-01 literal path: the registry lives in the subpackage internal/driver/registry/ (package registry), not the literal file internal/driver/registry.go — that file would be package driver, which must import neither internal/model (ADR-0012, enforced by TestNoWireImports over every *.go in internal/driver/) nor internal/driver/inprocess (import cycle: inprocess imports driver), yet Default(cfg) needs model.ProviderConfig and the inprocess constructors. S04 recorded and passed verification with the identical divergence class. Still under internal/driver/, still covered by the spec's `go test ./internal/driver/...` command. (captain-proceed.md disposition 2.i)

AC-01 symbol name: registry.Default(cfg) instead of DefaultRegistry(cfg) — the qualified name registry.Default is the equivalence (avoids the registry.DefaultRegistry stutter); the equivalence is stated in the package doc comment and here explicitly: registry.Default IS AC-01's DefaultRegistry. (captain-proceed.md disposition 2.ii)
Touchpoint internal/model/registry_test.go does not exist on this branch — nothing to delete; verified by ls/grep before and during implementation. (captain-proceed.md disposition 2.iii)

AC-01/R-02 'sworn capabilities re-pointed': no capabilities verb existed in cmd/sworn before this slice (grep-verified), so the verb is CREATED rendering registry enumeration — satisfying the intent; R-02's 'breaks consumers' premise was vacuous. (captain-proceed.md disposition 2.iv)

Coach ack flag (a) — deprecation-warning duplication ACCEPTED, not deduped: on the post-S06 loop path a deprecated openai-responses/ ID warns once at Registry.Resolve (injectable Warnf) and once more when InProcess.Dispatch re-resolves through NewClient (stderr). Each surface warns exactly once per resolution, the duplicate exists only for the one release the alias survives, and deduping would require cross-package warn-state (hidden coupling for a transient alias).

sworn llm-check -type ac-satisfaction could not dispatch in this session: 'no model configured (set --model or $SWORN_MODEL)' — no provider key, no SWORN_MODEL, no proxy login in this environment. Manual AC-to-test cross-check performed instead; every AC maps to named tests (see delivered[] evidence). Same class as the S04-recorded llm-check skip.

sworn coverage (role-prompt coverage-gate reference implementation) does not exist as a verb in this branch's binary ('unknown command "coverage"'); the manual AC-to-test matrix above stands in. scripts/release-verify.sh's known 'spec.md missing' false-FAIL on spec-v1 slices applies to this slice; per project guidance no spec.md was manufactured.
